name: Algo25 Package Examples
description: Create runnable examples for the @algorandfoundation/algokit-algo25 package. IMPORTANT: After implementing each example, you MUST run it to verify it works: `npx tsx <example>.ts`. The algo25 package provides mnemonic encoding/decoding utilities with NO LocalNet requirement - all examples work standalone without any network dependencies.

userStories.title: Infrastructure Setup - Verify Shared Utilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify shared utilities work and set up the algo25 examples folder structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify examples/shared/utils.ts exists and exports: printHeader(), printStep(), printInfo(), printSuccess(), printError(), formatBytes(), formatHex()
Create examples/algo25 folder if it doesn't exist
Verify the root examples/package.json includes @algorandfoundation/algokit-algo25 dependency
Verify the root examples/tsconfig.json has correct ESM module settings
npm install runs without errors in examples directory
TypeScript compiles without errors: npx tsc --noEmit
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - algo25 package is pure utilities

userStories.title: Mnemonic Generation Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use mnemonicFromSeed() to convert a 32-byte seed into a 25-word Algorand mnemonic.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algo25/01-mnemonic-from-seed.ts
Import mnemonicFromSeed from @algorandfoundation/algokit-algo25
Generate a random 32-byte seed using crypto.getRandomValues()
Demonstrate mnemonicFromSeed() to convert seed to 25-word mnemonic
Display the 25 words (24 data words + 1 checksum word)
Explain the 11-bit encoding scheme in output (256 bits = 24 words, checksum = 1 word)
Verify determinism: call mnemonicFromSeed() twice with same seed and show identical output
Display the seed in hex format for reference
Run the example with `npx tsx algo25/01-mnemonic-from-seed.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - pure utility function

userStories.title: Seed Recovery Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use seedFromMnemonic() to convert a 25-word mnemonic back to its original 32-byte seed.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algo25/02-seed-from-mnemonic.ts
Import mnemonicFromSeed and seedFromMnemonic from @algorandfoundation/algokit-algo25
Generate a random 32-byte seed and convert to mnemonic
Demonstrate seedFromMnemonic() to recover the seed from the mnemonic
Show round-trip verification: seedFromMnemonic(mnemonicFromSeed(seed)) equals original seed
Display hex representation of both original and recovered seeds
Use arrayEqual or compare bytes to verify equality
Run the example with `npx tsx algo25/02-seed-from-mnemonic.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - pure utility function

userStories.title: Secret Key to Mnemonic Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use secretKeyToMnemonic() to convert a 64-byte Algorand secret key to a mnemonic.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algo25/03-secret-key-to-mnemonic.ts
Import secretKeyToMnemonic and mnemonicFromSeed from @algorandfoundation/algokit-algo25
Create a 64-byte secret key (seed + public key, as Algorand uses)
Demonstrate secretKeyToMnemonic() to convert the 64-byte secret key to mnemonic
Explain that it extracts the first 32 bytes (seed portion) from the secret key
Compare with calling mnemonicFromSeed() directly on the first 32 bytes
Show that both methods produce identical mnemonics
Display the secret key structure: bytes 0-31 (seed) and bytes 32-63 (public key)
Run the example with `npx tsx algo25/03-secret-key-to-mnemonic.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - pure utility function

userStories.title: Master Derivation Key Functions Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use the master derivation key alias functions and understand their equivalence to the core functions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algo25/04-master-derivation-key.ts
Import mnemonicToMasterDerivationKey, masterDerivationKeyToMnemonic, seedFromMnemonic, and mnemonicFromSeed from @algorandfoundation/algokit-algo25
Generate a random 32-byte seed (master derivation key)
Demonstrate masterDerivationKeyToMnemonic() to convert MDK to mnemonic
Demonstrate mnemonicToMasterDerivationKey() to convert mnemonic back to MDK
Show equivalence: masterDerivationKeyToMnemonic(mdk) === mnemonicFromSeed(mdk)
Show equivalence: mnemonicToMasterDerivationKey(mn) equals seedFromMnemonic(mn)
Explain that these are convenience aliases for wallet derivation workflows
Run the example with `npx tsx algo25/04-master-derivation-key.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - pure utility functions (aliases)

userStories.title: Error Handling Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to handle errors when working with mnemonic functions, including invalid words, bad checksums, and wrong seed lengths.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algo25/05-error-handling.ts
Import FAIL_TO_DECODE_MNEMONIC_ERROR_MSG, NOT_IN_WORDS_LIST_ERROR_MSG, mnemonicFromSeed, and seedFromMnemonic from @algorandfoundation/algokit-algo25
Demonstrate NOT_IN_WORDS_LIST_ERROR_MSG error: pass a mnemonic with an invalid word (e.g., 'invalidword')
Demonstrate FAIL_TO_DECODE_MNEMONIC_ERROR_MSG error: pass a mnemonic with valid words but invalid checksum
Demonstrate RangeError for wrong seed length: pass a seed that is not 32 bytes to mnemonicFromSeed()
Show proper try/catch patterns for each error case
Display the error constants and their values
Show how to check error.message against the constants for programmatic handling
Run the example with `npx tsx algo25/05-error-handling.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - demonstrates error conditions

userStories.title: Verification Script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a script to run all algo25 package examples and verify they work.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algo25/verify-all.sh bash script
Script runs each example in order (01 through 05)
Script reports success/failure for each example
Script exits with non-zero code if any example fails
All 5 examples pass when running ./verify-all.sh
Update root examples/verify-all.sh to include algo25 examples if not already
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - just runs TypeScript examples
